summary: Dissolve the genre value shape into nested members at the embedded locus (0004): extract/kind/read/display fold, leaf addressing becomes member addressing.

new.description: The fold end-to-end (replaces the retired tests/genre_leaf.rs): a kind's inner-layer template extracts nested members at the embedded locus — own identity/fields/prose/edges (representation.md 'member'/'nesting') — opt-in per block, member addressing (member + child path) where leaf addressing used to be, undeclared content stays raw.

edit.description: Dissolve the genre value shape into nested members. Retire GenreValue(208)/GenreCollections(193)/LeafAddress(259)/addressed_leaves(236)/genre_leaves(364)/parse_genre_info(572)/parse_genre_value(592) as concepts; reshape Features.genres(321) to hold nested members carrying the embedded locus (representation.md 'locus': locus is binary file|embedded — introduce the embedded-locus notion; UnitShape{File,Directory} at kind.rs:116 is a different axis, untouched). FencedBlock(179)/Section(158) survive (raw substrate + the separate section_contains concern). The ts_rs/JsonSchema derive at the retired type ripples to the SDK bindings.

Genre(166)/CustomKind.genres(88-94)/fold_genres(269-285) dissolve into a `template` per inner layer — child kind plus embedded addressing per the parent's format (representation.md 'kind'/'nesting') — producing nested members. Reshape from_kind_fact_row(219-242) to restore the nested-member templates from the lock (today it drops genres to a fixed primitive set — the gap this closes). UnitShape untouched.

Leaf addressing becomes member addressing (0004 Consequences). Citation.target(107-114), resolve_target_species(149-192)/Species::Leaf(163), ParsedLeaf/parse_leaf_address(561-581), resolve_leaf(593-613), impact_leaf(624-684), narrate_citers(690-726), sibling_leaves(975-997), context 'Genre slots'(900-970), disclose_coverage(735) all address nested members over Features' new shape rather than Features::genre_leaves. why/requirements/floor_binding (member/requirement grain) untouched.
render_genre(37)/value_anchor(92)/leaf_anchor(98)/capitalize(106) — the whole per-genre display module — render nested members at member-address anchors, not GenreValue leaves.

The CLI help documenting the `<member>/<genre>/<key>/<field-path>` address form (main.rs:197-199) and the citations wiring (main.rs:479, an empty Vec today, no producer) follow member addressing. Shared with MODE-ROOT-MEMBER-FIELD (hence serialized). Stale architecture cites in this region ride the touch (rust.md exit clause).

Surface the nested-member shape in the generated JSON Schema (the prose-authoring keystroke path) where the retired genre value shape was surfaced. Shared with SATISFIER-KIND-CLAUSE (serialized via the blockedBy chain).

Update the `genres: Vec::new()` test-node stub (engine.rs:505) to the reshaped Features field. Engine stays genre-blind; RequireSections/SectionContains (engine.rs:368-402) are the separate section concern, untouched. Shared with SATISFIER-KIND-CLAUSE (serialized).

Update the `genres: Vec::new()` test-node stub (graph.rs:1021) to the reshaped Features field. degree/satisfier/routes_to logic is the separate satisfier-kind concern, untouched. Shared with SATISFIER-KIND-CLAUSE (serialized).

tests.asserts: The former leaf-grain walk (read_verbs.rs:170-203) addresses nested members, not leaves; the impact snapshot regenerates to member addressing (insta manages the .snap).
The fenced-primitive substrate still extracts (FencedBlock survives); what was the genre-value assertion (extract_equivalence.rs:133-176) now asserts nested-member extraction.
The Features test-node stub (graph.rs:613-617) constructs against the reshaped field; satisfier/degree assertions unaffected.
See files.new — the fold end-to-end at the embedded locus with member addressing.

acceptance: The genre value shape is gone: a kind's inner-layer template extracts nested members at the embedded locus (identity/fields/prose/edges), the contract layer ranges over them as over top-level members, and read verbs address members not leaves — no parallel value shape survives (representation.md 'nesting'/'locus', decision 0004).
notes: Derives 0004, the final slice of d5d1b37 (spec cursor advances). ATOMIC, not the predicted chain: clippy -D warnings forbids the transient unused code any stage-split creates (new shape unused pre-consumer, old unused post-abandon), and extract's type change is compile-coupled to kind/read/display. blockedBy MODE (itself blockedBy SATISFIER) — shares schema/engine/graph with SATISFIER, main.rs with MODE. section_contains/Section and satisfier/degree logic are separate concerns, untouched.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest — nothing moved this window.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs still pins SDK `^0.0.2` (0.0.3 published) — a release-owned bump, not this sweep.
